policy.purpose: Define the fail-closed, no-feature-loss capability boundary for the dependency-sovereignty program before any implementation or cutover.
policy.preservation_rule: Every accepted public API, generic extension point, feature/profile, binary, format, protocol, platform behavior, diagnostic, downstream integration, and documented user journey remains available unless a separate owner decision explicitly changes product scope.
policy.unknown_rule: Missing or inconclusive evidence is UNKNOWN_BLOCKING. It may not be interpreted as unused, unsupported, equivalent, or safe to remove.
policy.cutover_rule: A replacement remains BLOCKED_PENDING_EVIDENCE until CAP A2 baselines, VER A1 invariant evidence, VER A2 no-mock E2E evidence, campaign-specific parity, rollback, graph, security, and owner gates all pass.
policy.performance_rule: A smaller graph is not permission to regress correctness, interoperability, cancellation, security, accessibility, latency tails, throughput, memory, compile time, binary size, or user ergonomics.
policy.test_rule: Implementation beads must supply focused unit tests for normal, empty, boundary, maximum, malformed, error, panic where applicable, cancellation, race, shutdown, leak, and quiescence states plus property, differential, fuzz, model, or independent-vector evidence selected by risk.
policy.e2e_rule: Every user-visible, service, wire, persisted-format, security, or operational change must name a real no-mock scenario in the dependency-sovereignty suite and retain structured redacted logs with deterministic replay.
policy.deletion_rule: This registry grants no permission to delete source, tests, fixtures, formats, features, APIs, or compatibility data.

capabilities.title: Root public exports, generic traits, errors, and blessed entry points
capabilities.input_semantics: All downstream-compilable root modules, reexports, macros, generic bounds, public errors, and documented entry symbols.
capabilities.output_semantics: The same or better source-level usability and semantic behavior after each replacement.
capabilities.error_semantics: Public error variants, Display text, sources, and stable diagnostics remain mapped; incompatible narrowing is forbidden without an owner ADR.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Public calls retain documented boundedness and do not acquire hidden runtimes, globals, or background tasks.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: The root-export map is not item-level rustdoc compatibility proof and does not authorize removal of unlisted downstream generic uses.

capabilities.title: Cx, scopes, regions, tasks, cancellation, obligations, and quiescence
capabilities.input_semantics: Explicit Cx capabilities, budgets, policies, scopes, task bodies, cancellation requests, and obligation lifecycle events.
capabilities.output_semantics: Deterministic ownership, bounded admission, outcome classification, complete drain, and quiescent region close.
capabilities.error_semantics: Cancellation, timeout, failure, panic, and shutdown stay distinguishable and retain stable ASUP diagnostics where exposed.
capabilities.resource_semantics: No task or obligation leaks; work, memory, handles, and wakeups remain budget-accounted.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A dependency-level equivalence test does not prove global scheduler fairness, leak freedom, or all interleavings.

capabilities.title: Structured-concurrency scope, spawn, join, and race macros
capabilities.input_semantics: Rust syntax accepted by scope!, spawn!, join!, race!, and related structured-concurrency macros.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent expansions, type inference, hygiene, evaluation order, and compile-time diagnostics.
capabilities.error_semantics: Invalid syntax produces deterministic, located compile errors rather than panics or opaque expansion failures.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Generated code preserves structured ownership and does not add allocations or tasks beyond the explicit operation.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Passing current macro examples does not prove arbitrary downstream syntax or compiler-version compatibility.

capabilities.title: Deterministic LabRuntime, DPOR, replay, minimization, and scenario execution
capabilities.input_semantics: Seeds, scenario documents, schedules, fault plans, virtual time, invariants, and replay receipts.
capabilities.output_semantics: Reproducible traces, minimized counterexamples, stable invariant outcomes, and exact replay commands.
capabilities.error_semantics: Parse, model, invariant, replay-drift, and resource-limit failures remain distinct and actionable.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Exploration is explicitly bounded by steps, branches, memory, time, and corpus size.
security_invariants: replay does not gain ambient authority
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Deterministic coverage is bounded and does not by itself prove all production schedules or service interoperability.

capabilities.title: Browser/Wasm runtime, I/O, trace, and deterministic profiles
capabilities.input_semantics: Browser event, stream, storage, worker, entropy, deterministic-mode, and package boundary inputs.
capabilities.output_semantics: Profile-specific runtime, I/O, trace, and deterministic behavior without claiming unsupported native parity.
capabilities.error_semantics: DOM/JS boundary failures are typed, contextual, and do not silently downgrade requested capabilities.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Listeners, JS handles, streams, transactions, and workers are bounded and released on cancellation or drop.
cancellation_invariants: pending browser operations settle or abort
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A browser compile profile is not behavioral parity with native runtime or proof for untested browsers.

capabilities.title: Tower Service trait compatibility without a Tokio runtime edge
capabilities.input_semantics: Tower-shaped Service requests, readiness polling, errors, and futures.
capabilities.output_semantics: Trait-compatible adapters backed by asupersync ownership and cancellation semantics.
capabilities.error_semantics: Readiness, call, cancellation, and service errors stay observable without runtime substitution.
capabilities.resource_semantics: The adapter adds no executor and does not orphan in-flight calls.
security_invariants: no Tokio runtime in core graph
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Trait compilation alone does not prove behavior of arbitrary third-party Tower middleware.

capabilities.title: Stable ASUP error codes, structured tracing, redaction, and operator remediation
capabilities.input_semantics: Runtime failures, spans, fields, verbosity/filter configuration, PII patterns, and operator context.
capabilities.output_semantics: Stable leading ASUP tokens, structured events, deterministic CLI diagnostics, remediation links, and redacted forensic records.
capabilities.error_semantics: Logging failure never masks the primary error; unknown codes and secret leakage fail contracts.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Disabled instrumentation remains near-zero cost; enabled queues and fields are bounded.
security_invariants: diagnostics do not expose secret material
cancellation_invariants: terminal cancellation and shutdown events are emitted once
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Registry consistency does not prove every runtime failure is coded or every external log sink is secure.

capabilities.title: Unit, lab, property, fuzz, sparse-feature, cross-target, and real-service proof profiles
capabilities.input_semantics: Risk-tagged invariants, fixtures, feature cells, targets, service versions, failure plans, seeds, and replay commands.
capabilities.output_semantics: Admitted evidence with stable capability/scenario/step IDs, provenance, normalized outcomes, logs, timings, redaction result, and no-claim boundary.
capabilities.error_semantics: BLOCKED and UNSUPPORTED are explicit; timeout, infrastructure, assertion, leak, and schema failures are not conflated.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Every lane declares time, memory, process, service, corpus, and remote-execution envelopes.
security_invariants: no local fallback for required RCH proof
cancellation_invariants: teardown proves no leaked task, process, handle, or service
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A green focused lane proves only its declared surface, not broad workspace health or release readiness.

capabilities.title: Profiled marginal dependency, native-edge, duplicate-version, and graph-budget accounting
capabilities.input_semantics: Pinned Cargo metadata for synthesized consumer profiles, targets, features, dependency kinds, and package IDs.
capabilities.output_semantics: Reproducible marginal package/version/native/build-script/duplicate deltas and ranked cutover forecasts.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unknown target, package ambiguity, stale metadata, unsupported profile, and graph drift fail closed.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Graph generation is deterministic, bounded by Cargo metadata size, and excluded from production graphs.
cancellation_invariants: tool interruption leaves no claimed evidence artifact
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Graph cost does not prove replacement correctness, security, performance, or product value.

capabilities.title: Published-crate and FrankenSuite downstream compile, install, and workflow compatibility
capabilities.input_semantics: Published package, path consumer, feature selections, minimum versions, downstream generic types, and real application workflows.
capabilities.output_semantics: Successful resolution, compile, install, execution, migration, and rollback with unchanged accepted capabilities.
capabilities.error_semantics: Resolver, feature, API, packaging, runtime, and migration failures retain exact provenance.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Consumer graphs and binaries stay within ratcheted budgets without hidden native or executor edges.
cancellation_invariants: downstream shutdown and cleanup remain structured
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A curated portfolio cannot enumerate every private downstream use; generic extension surfaces remain first-class.

capabilities.title: Reactor and runtime hash-map semantics
capabilities.input_semantics: Token, descriptor, registration, and reactor lookup keys including collision and churn cases.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent insert, replace, lookup, remove, iteration-independent, and capacity behavior.
capabilities.error_semantics: Allocation and invariant failures do not become silent registration loss.
capabilities.resource_semantics: No unbounded tombstone/capacity retention or latency-tail regression under churn.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Two known call sites do not rule out behavior coupled through generic types or platform-specific reactor paths.

capabilities.title: Benchmark CPU-count and host-identity provenance
capabilities.input_semantics: Logical/physical CPU topology, available parallelism, user/host identity, container/cgroup context, and privacy policy.
capabilities.output_semantics: Stable provenance sufficient to interpret performance results without leaking unnecessary identity.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unavailable or ambiguous host fields are explicit UNKNOWN values rather than fabricated defaults.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Collection is bounded, non-blocking in hot paths, and outside measured benchmark intervals.
security_invariants: identity fields are minimized and redacted
container limits are not confused with host totals
cancellation_invariants: metadata collection cannot strand benchmark processes
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: std::available_parallelism alone does not represent sockets, physical cores, cgroups, affinity, or user-facing provenance.

capabilities.title: Secure temporary files and directories for tests, benchmarks, and logs
capabilities.input_semantics: Temporary roots, prefixes, permissions, collision policy, persist/close choices, and cleanup failure injection.
capabilities.output_semantics: Unique, permission-safe files/directories with explicit lifetime and forensic paths when retained.
capabilities.error_semantics: Creation, permission, collision, persistence, close, and cleanup failures are visible and contextual.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Handles and disk usage are bounded; cleanup is idempotent and leaves evidence on failure.
security_invariants: no secret paths or contents in logs
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Moving tempfile to dev tiers must not silently disable production-adjacent benchmark or logging workflows.

capabilities.title: Public Stream ergonomics, async helpers, block_on, combinators, and panic boundaries
capabilities.input_semantics: Arbitrary downstream Future/Stream implementations, wakeups, fused state, size hints, combinators, blocking test workflows, and panic paths.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent poll order, wake behavior, item/error ordering, termination, pinning, extension ergonomics, and test executor behavior.
capabilities.error_semantics: Panics, cancellation, end-of-stream, and item errors remain distinct; no silent item loss.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Combinators are bounded or explicitly backpressured and avoid unnecessary allocation/wakeup churn.
cancellation_invariants: drop at every await point is safe
reserved items are committed or released
buffered work drains or is explicitly discarded
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: In-repo occurrence parity does not cover arbitrary downstream Stream implementations or unusual wake/pin behavior.

capabilities.title: Test-internal visibility attribute behavior
capabilities.input_semantics: The complete accepted make_pub attribute syntax and its stereotyped module/item placements.
capabilities.output_semantics: Identical cfg-dependent visibility, hygiene, spans, and downstream test access.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unsupported placement or syntax yields a located compile error.
capabilities.resource_semantics: No runtime cost; compile-time expansion remains bounded.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Known stereotyped uses do not justify accepting a smaller attribute grammar without inventory evidence.

capabilities.title: Stable-key preallocated slab storage and reuse
capabilities.input_semantics: Insert, vacant-entry, index, get/get_mut, remove, clear, iteration, reserve, and generation/reuse patterns from every production consumer.
capabilities.output_semantics: Stable live keys, deterministic ownership, exact removal, and no stale-record aliasing.
capabilities.error_semantics: Invalid/stale keys and capacity failure fail explicitly or remain unreachable by proved invariant.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Amortized allocation and retained capacity remain bounded under churn; no unbounded key growth.
security_invariants: stale key cannot access a new security-sensitive record
cancellation_invariants: cancelled registrations release slots exactly once
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Four known production consumers must be re-counted at implementation time; this row is not permission to ignore new consumers.

capabilities.title: Generic and protocol hex encoding/decoding
capabilities.input_semantics: Upper/lowercase ASCII hex, empty/odd/invalid inputs, arbitrary bytes, decode-to-slice, and all current public/security/protocol/database/trace uses.
capabilities.output_semantics: Byte-for-byte canonical encoding and exact accepted decoding semantics.
capabilities.error_semantics: Invalid character, odd length, and output-length failures remain typed with equivalent location/detail.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Checked lengths, bounded allocation, no quadratic behavior, and no partial-secret output on error.
cancellation_invariants: pure operation is atomic from caller perspective
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: RFC vectors alone do not prove all public error, persisted-format, or security call-site behavior.

capabilities.title: RFC 4648 Base64 engines across authentication, HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC-Web, and certificates
capabilities.input_semantics: Standard and URL-safe alphabets, padded and unpadded engines, empty/boundary/malformed input, streaming/protocol framing, and credential/certificate material.
capabilities.output_semantics: Byte-for-byte RFC 4648 compatibility for all four engines and every accepted protocol embedding.
capabilities.error_semantics: Invalid byte, padding, length, trailing-bit, and output-capacity failures remain strict and typed.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Checked expansion bounds, bounded allocation, scalar safe baseline, and fail-before-partial-secret publication.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Codec equivalence does not prove the security or framing correctness of each embedding protocol.

capabilities.title: Bounded ATP artifact version-token recognition
capabilities.input_semantics: Artifact metadata containing valid, absent, duplicated, boundary, Unicode-adjacent, and malformed version tokens.
capabilities.output_semantics: Exact current recognized version and match-location behavior with an honest finite scanner.
capabilities.error_semantics: Malformed, unsupported, ambiguous, or oversized artifacts fail deterministically.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Single-pass bounded scan with explicit maximum input and no regex engine.
security_invariants: untrusted artifact bytes cannot trigger unbounded work
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: This finite scanner is not a general regex replacement and must not narrow unrelated custom-pattern capability.

capabilities.title: UTC instants, Gregorian calendar, RFC3339, checked duration, serialization, and clock capability
capabilities.input_semantics: Full accepted DateTime surface, nanosecond epochs, offsets, leap/calendar boundaries, fractional precision, checked arithmetic, serde, database timestamps, and explicit clocks.
capabilities.output_semantics: Round-trip compatible public and persisted values, canonical RFC3339, deterministic CLI/report timestamps, and equivalent database conversions.
capabilities.error_semantics: Range, calendar, parse, offset, precision, overflow, and ambiguous-clock errors remain typed and contextual.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Parsing/formatting is linear and bounded; wall-clock access is explicit and absent from deterministic paths.
security_invariants: no ambient clock in deterministic logic
cancellation_invariants: time acquisition/formatting does not block cancellation
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A nanoseconds-to-RFC3339 helper is only a foundation and cannot justify removing chrono/time before full public and persisted parity.

capabilities.title: Arbitrary downstream Serde JSON, Bincode, and MessagePack codecs
capabilities.input_semantics: Arbitrary downstream Serialize/Deserialize types including borrowed data, maps, enums, options, numeric boundaries, recursion, unknown fields, and custom visitors.
capabilities.output_semantics: JSON, Bincode, and MessagePack variants remain usable with compatible bytes/data model where promised; purpose-built formats are additive.
capabilities.error_semantics: Format, type, depth, trailing-data, UTF-8, numeric, and I/O errors retain useful location/source context.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Depth, length, allocation, and streaming bounds are explicit; malformed inputs cannot cause unbounded resource use.
cancellation_invariants: streaming decode retains frame ownership on cancellation
pure encode/decode is atomic to caller
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Finite in-repo schemas are not a substitute for arbitrary downstream Serde capability.

capabilities.title: Versioned typed-symbol, snapshot, trace, replay, and migration formats
capabilities.input_semantics: Historical and current typed symbols, snapshots, trace headers/records, compressed/uncompressed streams, schema versions, unknown fields, truncation, and corruption.
capabilities.output_semantics: Deterministic canonical new artifacts plus explicit backward read/migration/rollback behavior for every accepted historical artifact.
capabilities.error_semantics: Version, schema, checksum, truncation, corruption, migration, and replay mismatch errors are stable and localized.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Streaming decode has explicit record/depth/size/allocation limits and bounded decompression.
cancellation_invariants: partial writes are not published as complete
replay cancellation leaves deterministic checkpoint state
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Canonicalizing future artifacts does not authorize dropping any accepted historical artifact without migration and rollback proof.

capabilities.title: Versioned runtime configuration with preserved TOML and additive canonical JSON
capabilities.input_semantics: All accepted TOML grammar/data, canonical JSON, defaults, includes if any, CLI/env/file precedence, duplicate/unknown fields, comments, paths, and versioning.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent typed configuration and precedence; TOML read/write remains available while JSON is additive and reversible where promised.
capabilities.error_semantics: Syntax, type, unknown-field, duplicate, version, precedence, path, and semantic validation errors retain spans and actionable diagnostics.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Config size, nesting, strings, arrays, and includes are bounded; parsing is deterministic.
security_invariants: config cannot gain ambient file/network authority
cancellation_invariants: partial migration does not replace source
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Adding JSON does not permit narrowing accepted TOML or losing comments/data during migration.

capabilities.title: Versioned deterministic scenarios with preserved YAML and additive JSON
capabilities.input_semantics: Entire accepted YAML corpus, anchors/tags/scalars as actually supported, additive JSON, scenario versions, faults, invariants, seeds, and authoring workflows.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent execute/explore/replay behavior and reversible migration without data loss.
capabilities.error_semantics: Syntax, schema, semantic, version, fixture, execution, and replay errors remain located and actionable.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Document size, nesting, collection count, steps, branches, and exploration resources are bounded.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A typed JSON schema is additive; it is not permission to delete or lossy-convert accepted YAML.

capabilities.title: Arbitrary downstream Protobuf messages, authoring, streaming, and gRPC wire interoperability
capabilities.input_semantics: Full accepted Protobuf scalar/wire types, packed fields, maps, oneofs, repeated/nested messages, unknown fields, groups policy, downstream-defined schemas, derives/codegen, length-delimited streaming, and gRPC framing.
capabilities.output_semantics: Cross-language compatible bytes, unknown-field/evolution policy, ergonomic downstream authoring, and streaming behavior.
capabilities.error_semantics: Malformed key, wire type, varint, length, depth, UTF-8, truncation, recursion, and output-capacity failures are typed and bounded.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Checked lengths, recursion/depth/field/message limits, bounded allocation, and streaming backpressure.
cancellation_invariants: stream cancellation releases buffers and reservations
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Finite in-repo schemas or OTLP messages cannot replace arbitrary downstream Protobuf capability.

capabilities.title: OTLP metrics, traces, logs, SDK/provider adapters, collector interop, and bounded export
capabilities.input_semantics: Metrics, traces, logs/events, resources, instrumentation scopes, temporality, aggregation, attributes, endpoints, TLS, auth, retries, timeouts, batching, third-party SDK/provider/exporter integration, and collector responses.
capabilities.output_semantics: Spec-compatible multi-signal OTLP with preserved ecosystem adapters, lineage, cardinality policy, PII redaction, and collector interoperability.
capabilities.error_semantics: Configuration, mapping, encode, transport, TLS/auth, throttle, retry, partial-success, shutdown, and redaction failures remain distinct.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Bounded batches, queues, attributes, cardinality, retries, memory, connections, and cancellation-aware shutdown.
security_invariants: shared redaction applies to every signal
no Tokio in metrics production graph
cancellation_invariants: cancel does not orphan batches or retry loops
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Metrics-only or generated-message agreement does not prove traces, logs, provider ecosystem, lifecycle, or real collector parity.

capabilities.title: Full user-supplied regex semantics plus safe fixed-detector fast paths and privacy redaction
capabilities.input_semantics: Complete accepted Unicode/bytes regex syntax, flags, classes, captures, boundaries, case folding, leftmost behavior, find/replace, zero-width progress, invalid patterns, and user PrivacyConfig patterns.
capabilities.output_semantics: Identical compile and matching semantics; fixed PII detectors may accelerate only equivalence-proved patterns.
capabilities.error_semantics: Compile errors remain typed, located, stable, and safe to display; match/resource limits fail explicitly.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Linear-time matching policy, bounded compiler/IR/cache/captures/memory, concurrent use, and cancellation checkpoints for large scans.
security_invariants: custom privacy patterns never silently ignored
adversarial input cannot trigger catastrophic backtracking
cancellation_invariants: long scans are bounded or cooperative
cache work cannot outlive owning context
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Fixed scanners are not a general regex engine and may never justify dropping custom user patterns.

capabilities.title: Complete NKey credentials, prefixes, signing, verification, Curve keys, JWT signer policy, and NATS interoperability
capabilities.input_semantics: Every public/seed/private/Curve prefix and encoded form, canonical Base32, CRC16-XMODEM, signing, verification, nonce challenge, JWT account/operator/user signer authorization, malformed and adversarial credentials.
capabilities.output_semantics: Byte-compatible canonical NKeys, misuse-resistant owned key types, equivalent signatures/verifications, signer policy, and real NATS/JWT authentication.
capabilities.error_semantics: Prefix, length, Base32, checksum, key-type, secret-disposition, signature, verification, and signer-policy failures are distinct and non-oracular.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Bounded parse/encode/sign/verify, zeroized secret state, no secret clones in ordinary APIs.
cancellation_invariants: authentication cancellation releases connection state and wipes transient secrets
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Seed decoding and nonce signing alone do not cover the full NKey format, Curve keys, JWT signer policy, downstream API, or security.

capabilities.title: Cross-protocol credentials, authentication configuration, secrecy, and redaction
capabilities.input_semantics: NATS token/user-password/NKey/JWT, HTTP Basic/Bearer, TLS certificates/keys, PostgreSQL SCRAM, Kafka SASL mechanisms, OTLP headers, and secret-source policy.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent successful authentication and explicit mechanism negotiation with uniform secret lifecycle/redaction.
capabilities.error_semantics: Missing, malformed, unsupported, rejected, expired, unauthorized, transport, and policy failures remain distinguishable without secret disclosure.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Credential sizes, KDF work, retries, handshakes, caches, and secret lifetime are bounded.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Passing one protocol's authentication does not establish another mechanism's security, downgrade, or lifecycle parity.

capabilities.title: Installed asupersync CLI command, configuration, trace, help, diagnostics, and exit contract
capabilities.input_semantics: Every command, subcommand, positional, long/short option, alias, default, repeated/conflicting/requiring group, value parser, OsString boundary, environment/config edge, color/width mode, and stdin/file input.
capabilities.output_semantics: Byte-level compatible or owner-approved improved help, usage, version, success output, diagnostics, ordering, accessibility, and exit status.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unknown/missing/conflicting/invalid options, file/config/format/runtime failures, and broken pipes retain deterministic stderr/stdout placement and stable exits.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Argument/help parsing is bounded and fast; trace/config operations retain streaming and file-size limits.
security_invariants: paths and artifacts are untrusted bounded input
cancellation_invariants: Ctrl-C and broken pipe terminate cleanly
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A command inventory is not parity until installed binaries, shell/OsString boundaries, accessibility, diagnostics, and real workflows pass.

capabilities.title: Installed ATP transfer CLI with full transport, security, resume, artifact, and diagnostic behavior
capabilities.input_semantics: All send/receive/inspect/transport/security/network/resume/tuning/artifact options, aliases, values, paths, addresses, credentials, environment, verbosity, and signal behavior.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent installed transfer workflows, deterministic help/diagnostics, progress/result artifacts, transport selection, and exit status.
capabilities.error_semantics: Argument, path, network, TLS, authentication, capability negotiation, transfer, integrity, cancellation, and resume failures remain distinguishable.
capabilities.resource_semantics: CLI parsing is bounded; transfer memory, descriptors, streams, retries, and progress logging retain explicit budgets.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Parsing parity does not prove real transfer, TLS, resume, cancellation, cleanup, or installed-package behavior.

capabilities.title: ATP daemon CLI, configuration, supervision, logging, signal, and lifecycle behavior
capabilities.input_semantics: All daemon options, config/env precedence, listen/security paths, verbosity/filtering, service mode, process/signal controls, and invalid combinations.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent help, startup report, readiness, structured logs, daemon supervision, graceful shutdown, restart behavior, and exits.
capabilities.error_semantics: Argument/config/bind/security/process/signal/runtime/shutdown failures retain stable diagnostics and remediation.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Listeners, workers, files, logs, timers, and child processes are budgeted and released at shutdown.
security_invariants: process identity and liveness checks resist PID reuse
cancellation_invariants: signals map to bounded structured drain
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: The atpd feature remains explicitly gated; registry inclusion does not claim production readiness.

capabilities.title: Offline tuner CLI, host provenance, benchmark ingestion, optimization reports, and logging
capabilities.input_semantics: Every tuner option, benchmark/profile artifact, host metadata field, verbosity/filter, algorithm/constraint, malformed data, and output destination.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent deterministic recommendations, provenance, human/machine reports, help, logs, and exits.
capabilities.error_semantics: Argument, artifact, schema, constraint, host, optimization, output, and logging failures stay actionable.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Input/candidate counts, optimization work, memory, threads, and output size are bounded and reported.
cancellation_invariants: interrupt leaves no falsely complete result
temporary outputs are atomic and cleaned
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Removing env_logger or host crates is blocked until verbosity, diagnostics, privacy, and report provenance are equal or better.

capabilities.title: One-stop async SQLite feature, API, transactions, cancellation, errors, packaging, and official FrankenSQLite path
capabilities.input_semantics: Open/config flags, paths/memory DBs, pool admission, statements, binds, rows, typed values, transactions, savepoints, checked SQL, timeouts, interrupts, cancellation races, errors, and downstream installation.
capabilities.output_semantics: Complete public API and behavior parity through a cycle-safe official integration, including query/transaction/cancel/restart/rollback user journeys.
capabilities.error_semantics: Open/config/prepare/bind/step/type/constraint/busy/interrupt/timeout/cancel/transaction/pool errors retain stable mapping and cleanup state.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Connections, blocking bridges, statements, caches, rows, transactions, threads, and memory are bounded and quiescent.
cancellation_invariants: pool state and region quiescence restored
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: FrankenSQLite currently depends on asupersync; it may not be added back as an asupersync dependency or used as an in-workspace oracle.

capabilities.title: Kafka protocol, producer, transactions, consumers, groups, security, compression, observability, and broker interoperability
capabilities.input_semantics: All accepted broker versions/APIs, flexible tags, metadata, RecordBatch fields/compression, producer acks/retries/idempotence/transactions, fetch/isolation, consumer groups/rebalances/offsets, TLS/SASL, configuration, errors, and metrics.
capabilities.output_semantics: Cross-broker compatible production, transactional atomicity, ordered delivery, consumption, group coordination, restart/recovery, security, and public API behavior.
capabilities.error_semantics: Protocol, version, routing, leader, auth, timeout, throttle, delivery, fencing, rebalance, offset, decode, resource, cancel, and shutdown failures remain typed.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Frames, batches, queues, connections, retries, in-flight requests, decompression, fetch buffers, group work, and metrics are bounded/backpressured.
security_invariants: TLS/SASL mechanisms and downgrade policy preserved
cancellation_invariants: group leave and connection shutdown bounded
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A wire codec or simple producer is not a Kafka client; librdkafka stays until the complete campaign and real broker matrix pass.

capabilities.title: TLS client/server, roots, PEM, X.509 validation, QUIC/ATP integration, pinning, and diagnostics
capabilities.input_semantics: TLS 1.2/1.3 configuration as supported, server names, native/webpki/custom roots, PEM certs/keys, chains, DER canonicality, times, signatures, KU/EKU/BasicConstraints, names, critical/duplicate extensions, pinning, alerts, resumption, ALPN, and all TLS/QUIC/ATP call paths.
capabilities.output_semantics: Standards-compliant authenticated channels, delegated standard validation, minimal owned DER residue only where non-delegable, equivalent pins/errors, and cross-implementation interop.
capabilities.error_semantics: Configuration, PEM/DER, chain, trust, name, time, signature, usage, extension, pin, handshake, alert, I/O, cancel, and shutdown failures remain precise and fail closed.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Certificate/chain/extension/handshake sizes, CPU, connections, buffers, and shutdown are bounded.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Owning SPKI extraction or a DER reader does not authorize replacing certificate validation or cryptography.

capabilities.title: Negotiated gzip, deflate, and Brotli HTTP response compression
capabilities.input_semantics: Accept-Encoding grammar, q-values, identity/wildcard, content-type/size policy, already-encoded responses, streaming bodies, raw DEFLATE/zlib/gzip, Brotli, malformed/truncated streams, and cross-implementation peers.
capabilities.output_semantics: RFC-compatible negotiation and byte streams for every accepted coding without altering status, headers, body semantics, or streaming backpressure.
capabilities.error_semantics: Negotiation, encoder, decoder/test-oracle, truncation, checksum, size-limit, I/O, cancellation, and unsupported-coding failures are explicit.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Window, dictionary, memory, CPU, expansion ratio, buffering, output size, and concurrent streams are bounded.
cancellation_invariants: no truncated body is presented as complete
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: DEFLATE parity does not cover Brotli; Brotli remains an accepted capability and incumbent until its own full campaign passes.

capabilities.title: LZ4 block/frame trace compression and replay compatibility
capabilities.input_semantics: Accepted LZ4 block/frame variants, historical trace artifacts, compressed/uncompressed streams, dictionaries/checksums if used, malformed/truncated data, and streaming boundaries.
capabilities.output_semantics: Byte/interoperability-compatible new traces and backward-readable/replayable historical artifacts.
capabilities.error_semantics: Header, version, block, checksum, length, truncation, expansion-limit, I/O, and replay failures remain distinct.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Block/window/output/expansion/memory limits and streaming buffers are bounded.
cancellation_invariants: partial compressed traces are not marked complete
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Round-tripping freshly generated data does not prove historical artifact or external implementation compatibility.

capabilities.title: Cross-platform signal registration, delivery, daemon control, and graceful shutdown
capabilities.input_semantics: Supported Unix signals, Windows console/control events, registration, multiple listeners, coalescing/order policy, masking, process signaling, permission/PID-reuse, repeated delivery, and teardown.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent event delivery and graceful/forced process lifecycle across supported platforms.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unsupported signal, registration, pipe/event, permission, not-found/PID-reuse, delivery, cancellation, and shutdown failures remain explicit.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Handlers, pipes/events, threads, descriptors, queues, and wakeups are bounded and released.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A Unix self-pipe implementation does not cover Windows control events or cross-process permission/liveness semantics.

capabilities.title: Cross-platform host/process CPU, memory, filesystem, liveness, and container-aware metrics
capabilities.input_semantics: System/process refresh requests, PIDs and identity, CPU/memory/disk units, cgroup/container limits, filesystems, liveness, freshness, unsupported fields, and permission errors.
capabilities.output_semantics: Unit-stable, freshness-tagged snapshots and reliable process control/benchmark/failure-bundle data across supported platforms.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unsupported, unavailable, stale, permission, parse, process-gone/PID-reused, overflow, and OS-call failures remain explicit.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Procfs/sysctl/Win32 reads, buffers, handles, refresh cadence, CPU overhead, and cache lifetime are bounded.
cancellation_invariants: monitor tasks stop at region close
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Linux procfs support alone does not justify dropping macOS/Windows fields or sysinfo.

capabilities.title: Cross-platform extended attributes and ATP metadata preservation
capabilities.input_semantics: Get/set/list/remove, names/values, missing/unsupported, symlink follow/no-follow, macOS position/options, size races, permissions, namespaces, and ATP transfer policy.
capabilities.output_semantics: Exact supported-platform xattr semantics and metadata preservation across real transfers.
capabilities.error_semantics: Missing, unsupported, permission, namespace, size-race, symlink, invalid-name/value, I/O, cancellation, and partial-transfer failures remain distinguishable.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Name/value/list sizes, retries, allocations, descriptors, and transfer metadata are bounded.
cancellation_invariants: cancelled metadata application has explicit partial-state/rollback
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A Linux wrapper does not cover macOS signatures/options, BSD support, symlink policy, or ATP metadata parity.

capabilities.title: Portable reactor polling and low-level socket configuration
capabilities.input_semantics: Descriptor/socket creation and flags, address families, options, bind/listen/connect/accept, readiness interests/events, one-shot/level/edge behavior, wake, deregistration, close races, errors, and platform quirks.
capabilities.output_semantics: Portable cancel-correct I/O and socket behavior with exact supported option/error semantics.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unsupported option, address, permission, would-block, interrupted, stale event, closed descriptor, wake, registration, cancellation, and OS errors remain mapped.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Descriptors, registrations, event buffers, wakes, syscalls, memory, and fairness are bounded.
security_invariants: socket flags applied atomically where required
cancellation_invariants: cancelled operations release registration and descriptor ownership
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: The plan currently favors a terminal KEEP/defer receipt; no partial platform implementation authorizes cutover.

capabilities.title: Mutex, RwLock, Condvar, guards, poison recovery, fairness, metrics, and lock ordering
capabilities.input_semantics: Lock/read/write/try/timed operations, guard map/downgrade behavior actually used, Condvar predicate/wait/timeout/notify, panic/poison, contention, fairness, ordering, metrics, and shutdown.
capabilities.output_semantics: Safe owned APIs with equivalent correctness and same-or-better throughput/tails/fairness/scaling across supported hosts.
capabilities.error_semantics: Poison/panic, timeout, cancellation-adjacent lifecycle, ordering violation, unsupported operation, and shutdown remain explicit.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Waiters, OS objects, metrics, memory, cache lines, CPU spin/park, latency tails, and 1/8/32/64-core scaling are gated.
cancellation_invariants: no guard held across prohibited await
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: A safe wrapper compiling is not enough; cutover requires complete API migration, concurrency proof, and full-axis performance acceptance.

capabilities.title: Concurrent ready/injection/wakeup queue semantics and safe experimental alternatives
capabilities.input_semantics: Push/pop, MPMC contention, empty/full state, wakeups, shutdown, fairness, ordering constraints, panic, cancellation, and producer/consumer churn at every current queue site.
capabilities.output_semantics: Equivalent correctness and no lost/duplicated work; incumbent remains unless a strictly safe alternative wins or ties all gates.
capabilities.error_semantics: Capacity/shutdown/invariant failures are explicit; no silent work loss.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Capacity, allocation, cache contention, CPU spin, latency tails, memory, and high-core scaling are bounded/measured.
cancellation_invariants: cancelled producers/consumers do not lose committed work
shutdown drains or explicitly returns queued ownership
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: The safe mutex-backed queue is an experiment; crossbeam-queue remains by default unless it wins or ties correctness and full performance gates.

capabilities.title: Per-structure cache-line padding and false-sharing policy
capabilities.input_semantics: Measured structures, field access patterns, cache topology, core/socket/CCX placement, 64/128-byte alignments, and workload matrices.
capabilities.output_semantics: Per-structure evidence-backed alignment decisions with no global padding assumption.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unsupported architecture/topology or noisy/inconclusive measurement yields DEFER/KEEP, not a claimed win.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Throughput, p50/p95/p99/p999, memory/RSS, cache misses, binary size, and scaling are measured.
cancellation_invariants: layout changes preserve all queue/lock/runtime lifecycle semantics
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: 128-byte padding is not a global rule; only measured per-structure changes may proceed.

capabilities.title: RaptorQ GF(256) scalar/portable-SIMD/intrinsics dispatch and correctness
capabilities.input_semantics: GF(256) symbols/matrices, misalignment/length tails, runtime feature detection, scalar/portable/intrinsic dispatch, corrupted data, and all RaptorQ transfer sizes.
capabilities.output_semantics: Bit-identical RFC6330 results across kernels and deterministic fallback on every supported CPU.
capabilities.error_semantics: Unsupported CPU, invalid dimensions, overflow, singular decode, corruption, and dispatch mismatch fail explicitly or fall back safely.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Bounds checks, allocations, memory bandwidth, cache behavior, throughput, tails, and high-core scaling are measured.
security_invariants: unsafe limited to ledgered measured kernels
cancellation_invariants: kernel work is bounded between cancellation checkpoints
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Portable SIMD may scalarize; performance claims require measured host-specific evidence and never replace correctness proof.

capabilities.title: NATS request/reply, pub/sub, JetStream, authentication, reconnect, and messaging lifecycle
capabilities.input_semantics: Server URLs, INFO/CONNECT/PING/PONG, pub/sub/request/reply, headers, payload limits, auth forms, reconnect, subscriptions, JetStream ack/dedup/flow control, timeouts, and shutdown.
capabilities.output_semantics: Protocol-compatible message ordering/delivery/ack behavior, cancellation-correct public APIs, and real NATS interoperability.
capabilities.error_semantics: Protocol, auth, permission, timeout, disconnect, reconnect, server, payload, ack, cancellation, and shutdown failures remain distinct.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Connections, subscriptions, pending messages, payloads, buffers, reconnects, heartbeats, and ack obligations are bounded.
cancellation_invariants: reconnect and shutdown tasks are owned
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Replacing NKey/Base64 helpers must not regress any wider NATS or JetStream lifecycle behavior.

capabilities.title: Native PostgreSQL and MySQL wire clients, authentication, typing, transactions, pooling, and cancellation
capabilities.input_semantics: Connection/config/auth including SCRAM, TLS, queries, prepared statements, binds/types/rows, transactions, pooling, timeouts, cancellation, notices/errors, and server-version behavior.
capabilities.output_semantics: Protocol-compatible query/transaction behavior, typed values, cancellation-correct pooling, and real database interoperability.
capabilities.error_semantics: DNS/connect/TLS/auth/protocol/server/type/query/transaction/pool/timeout/cancel/shutdown failures remain distinct and retain server detail safely.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Connections, statements, rows, buffers, result sizes, retries, pools, and server work are bounded/backpressured.
cancellation_invariants: server cancellation and client cleanup race-safe
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Dependency helper cutovers cannot narrow PostgreSQL/MySQL public types, authentication, or service-version behavior.

capabilities.title: Native QUIC, HTTP/3, ATP transfer, RaptorQ repair, resume, congestion, and forensic evidence
capabilities.input_semantics: QUIC/TLS config, packets/frames/streams/datagrams, HTTP/3, ATP manifests/chunks/deltas/resume/repair/congestion/path selection, credentials/pins, file metadata, loss/reorder/corruption, and shutdown.
capabilities.output_semantics: Interoperable authenticated transport and exact ATP file/object transfer with integrity, resume, repair, backpressure, diagnostics, and deterministic forensic artifacts.
capabilities.error_semantics: Handshake, crypto, protocol, flow-control, congestion, path, integrity, artifact, filesystem, timeout, cancellation, peer, and shutdown failures remain classified.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Connections, streams, packets, symbols, repair rounds, memory, files, timers, bandwidth, CPU, and logs are budgeted.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: Helper-code equivalence does not prove native QUIC, HTTP/3, ATP, congestion, security, or transfer performance.

capabilities.title: No-mock real-service and installed-user workflow coverage with detailed redacted replay logs
capabilities.input_semantics: Pinned services/tools, isolated ports/roots, source revisions, features/targets, fixtures, failure plans, canary secrets, run/scenario/step IDs, and deterministic replay parameters.
capabilities.output_semantics: Validated summary.json, events.ndjson, per-step stdout/stderr, provenance, normalized outcomes/errors/cancellation/resource state, timings, redaction scan, cleanup receipt, and exact replay command.
capabilities.error_semantics: Assertion, schema, setup, service, timeout, cancellation, cleanup, leak, redaction, RCH, BLOCKED, and UNSUPPORTED states remain explicit; silent skip is forbidden.
capabilities.resource_semantics: Every scenario declares time/memory/process/service/disk/network limits and cleans all owned resources.
capabilities.no_claim_boundary: This registry names the required E2E contract; CAP A1 does not falsely claim the future runner or service evidence already exists.

cli_surface_inventory.snapshot_rule: Until CLI A1 publishes semantic argv/help/error goldens, hashes of all six parser/command sources plus the shared exit registry are the exhaustive option, alias, default, value, help, and exit drift boundary. Any source change requires explicit review and refresh; a hash match is inventory evidence, not behavioral parity.

environment_variables.name: CLICOLOR_FORCE
environment_variables.semantics: Force color even when output is not a TTY

environment_variables.name: ATP_RQ_AUTH_KEY_HEX
environment_variables.semantics: Hex-encoded 32-byte legacy RQ symbol-auth key fallback

environment_variables.name: HOME
environment_variables.semantics: User-home path for ATP shell/install workflows

environment_variables.name: PROGRAMDATA
environment_variables.semantics: Windows ATP daemon root/config path input

cli_surface_inventory.help_contract: Preserve deterministic command order, usage, option spelling, short flags, aliases, defaults, value names, constraints, width/color/accessibility behavior, stdout/stderr routing, version text, and shell/OsString boundaries.
cli_surface_inventory.no_claim_boundary: Full-source hashes detect drift but do not prove argv/help/error parity; CLI A1 and CLI A11 own executable goldens and installed no-mock workflows.

api_surface_snapshot.semantic_projection: kind TAB name TAB comma-joined-feature-flags TAB cfg NEWLINE in artifact order
api_surface_snapshot.selector_policy: every root export must match at least one selector

diagnostic_inventory.cutover_rule: Every registered code remains mapped; code removal or semantic reassignment requires its own owner decision and migration.

downstream_probe_policy.local_probe_rule: When /dp is mounted, every listed repository and Cargo.toml must exist and the manifest must contain an asupersync dependency declaration.
downstream_probe_policy.remote_absence_rule: An RCH worker without /dp validates registry structure only and may not claim downstream compatibility, freshness, or green portfolio evidence.
downstream_probe_policy.canonical_probe_command: for manifest in /dp/*/Cargo.toml; do rg -n 'asupersync' "$manifest"; done
downstream_probe_policy.no_claim_boundary: Presence of a dependency declaration is inventory evidence only; CAP A2 and VER A5 own clean synthesized-consumer builds and real downstream workflows.

validation.focused_proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${RCH_TARGET_BASE:-${TMPDIR:-/tmp}}/rch_target_dependency_capability_registry" cargo test -p asupersync --test dependency_capability_registry_contract -- --nocapture
negative_fixtures: missing or invalid downstream manifest on the authoritative portfolio host
validation.no_claim_boundary: This artifact proves inventory structure and current source/tracker drift checks only. It does not prove implementation parity, runtime correctness, performance, service interoperability, broad workspace health, release readiness, or permission to cut over or delete anything.
